GameTime.brief: A class that manages the in-game date, time, and lighting conditions within the game world.
GameTime.details: The GameTime class is responsible for tracking and updating the in-game calendar and lighting state, including the current hour, day, month, and year, as well as the phase of sunlight (Night, Sunrise, Sunset, Day). It provides methods to retrieve the names of the current day and month, and to generate a human-readable string for the current day with an ordinal suffix. The constructor initializes the game time based on real-world system time, converting it into the game's internal calendar and lighting conditions. The update method advances the in-game clock by one hour, updating date and lighting states accordingly, and can notify other game systems of time-dependent events. The class enforces immutability by privatizing its copy constructor and assignment operator to prevent unintended copying. It is integral to systems such as weather, environment, and event scheduling, ensuring that visual and gameplay elements are synchronized with the current in-game time and lighting conditions, thereby supporting a dynamic and immersive game environment.
GameTime.notes: The class employs the Sun enumeration to categorize different lighting phases, facilitating consistent environmental behavior and visual effects. Its design emphasizes encapsulation and controlled time progression, making it suitable for simulation of realistic day-night cycles and calendar-based gameplay mechanics.
GameTime.rationale: GameTime exists to provide a centralized and consistent management of in-game temporal data, enabling various game systems to synchronize their behavior with the current date, time, and lighting conditions. By converting real-world system time into the game’s calendar, it ensures that the game environment reflects real-time progression, supporting features like seasonal changes, time-based events, and immersive storytelling.
GameTime.name: GameTime

Sun.brief: Enumeration defining the various phases of daylight based on the sun's position in the game environment.
Sun.details: The GameTime::Sun enum specifies the different states of the sun's position that influence lighting, visuals, and gameplay mechanics within the system. It includes Night for periods with no sunlight, Sunrise and Sunset for transitional phases, and Day for full daylight conditions. This categorization helps in managing environmental effects, character behaviors, and visual rendering based on the time of day.
Sun.notes: Ensure that transitions between states are handled smoothly to avoid abrupt visual changes. This enum is often used in conjunction with time progression systems to update environmental states accordingly.
Sun.rationale: The Sun enum provides a clear and structured way to categorize the different lighting conditions based on the sun's position, facilitating consistent environmental behavior and visual effects throughout the game system.
Sun.name: Sun

hour.brief: Represents the current in-game hour of the day within the game's 24-hour cycle.
hour.details: The 'hour' variable in the GameTime class tracks the current hour in the game's simulated 24-hour day. Initialized to zero, it is regularly updated, typically once per in-game tick, to reflect the progression of time. This variable is integral to controlling time-dependent game mechanics, such as triggering events at specific hours, adjusting environmental lighting to simulate day and night, and managing shop or NPC operating hours. During game initialization, it is often set based on the real-world system time or synchronized periodically to ensure consistency between real and in-game time, supporting immersive and time-sensitive gameplay experiences.
hour.notes: The 'hour' variable is stored as an integer and is updated in synchronization with the game's time progression system. Its value influences various game behaviors and is accessed frequently across different modules.
hour.rationale: This variable exists to facilitate time-based game mechanics, enabling the game to simulate a dynamic day-night cycle and enforce time-specific events, thereby enhancing realism and gameplay depth.
hour.name: hour

day.brief: Stores the current day within the in-game month.
day.details: The 'day' variable represents the current day number within the ongoing month in the game time system, starting from zero. It is initialized to zero when a new game time instance is created and is incremented as days pass in the game world. This variable is utilized to determine the day's name, generate date strings with appropriate suffixes, and reset at the start of each new month. It works in conjunction with other time components to maintain an accurate in-game calendar, supporting features like date display and time-based events.
day.notes: The 'day' variable is stored as an integer and is typically reset at the start of each month. Its value influences various date-related functions and string representations within the game.
day.rationale: This variable exists to track the progression of days within the current month, enabling the game to simulate a realistic calendar system and support date-dependent gameplay mechanics.
day.name: day

month.brief: Represents the current month within the game year as an integer value.
month.details: The 'month' variable in the GameTime class stores the current month number, starting from zero, and is initialized during object creation. It is derived from the total elapsed days calculated from the system time, allowing the game to track the progression of months throughout the in-game year. This variable is used for various purposes, including retrieving the month's name, influencing weather patterns, and triggering seasonal events. It is updated regularly as time advances, and resets at the beginning of a new year to ensure consistent month tracking within the game world.
month.notes: The 'month' variable is typically stored as an integer index (0-11) corresponding to months of the year. It is not directly involved in control flow but serves as a reference point for other game mechanics that depend on the current month.
month.rationale: This variable exists to facilitate month-based calculations and behaviors, supporting features like seasonal changes, weather variations, and time-dependent events, thereby enhancing the realism and dynamic nature of the game environment.
month.name: month

year.brief: Stores the current in-game calendar year within the game time simulation.
year.details: The `GameTime::year` variable maintains the current year in the game's internal calendar system. It is initialized to zero but is typically set based on the system time during game startup, reflecting the starting point of the in-game timeline. As the game progresses, this variable is incremented whenever a full year passes, which is determined by the accumulated months in the simulation. It is used in various time-dependent features such as displaying the current year to the player, triggering year-based events, and managing game state transitions that depend on the passage of years.
year.notes: The variable is updated in synchronization with the month count, ensuring consistency between months and years in the simulation. It is stored as an integer for simplicity and performance, with no fractional components.
year.rationale: This variable exists to provide an accessible and straightforward representation of the in-game calendar year, supporting features that depend on the passage of years and enabling the game to simulate a realistic timeline.
year.name: year

sunlight.brief: Indicates the current phase of sunlight or time of day in the game environment.
sunlight.details: The `sunlight` variable holds the current phase of the day, such as Night, Sunrise, Day, or Sunset, as defined by the `GameTime::Sun` enumeration. It is initialized during game startup based on the system time and is periodically updated to reflect the sun's position in the virtual environment. This variable plays a crucial role in influencing game mechanics like character regeneration, environmental effects, and visual rendering, providing a consistent indicator of the current daylight status within the game world.
sunlight.notes: The variable is typically stored as an enum value, allowing for efficient comparisons and updates. Its value is often synchronized with environmental changes and time progression logic.
sunlight.rationale: The `sunlight` variable exists to represent the current time-of-day state, enabling game systems to adapt behavior and visuals according to whether it is night, sunrise, day, or sunset, thereby enhancing realism and gameplay dynamics.
sunlight.name: sunlight

GameTime::GameTime(std::time_t system_ti.brief: Initializes a GameTime object by converting a given system time into the game's internal calendar and lighting state.
GameTime::GameTime(std::time_t system_ti.details: This constructor transforms a system time value into the game's internal time representation by calculating the total number of game pulses elapsed since a fixed reference point. It first determines the total hours passed by subtracting a base offset and dividing by the number of pulses per second. Using modular arithmetic with predefined constants for day, month, and year lengths, it extracts the current hour, day, month, and year within the game calendar. It also sets the current sunlight phase—Night, Sunrise, Day, or Sunset—based on the computed hour, aligning environmental lighting with the in-game time of day. The process ensures synchronization with the game's pulse-based timing system, providing a consistent and immersive time environment within the game world.

params.system_time: The system time in seconds since the epoch, serving as the reference point for calculating the in-game time.

GameTime::GameTime(std::time_t system_ti.returns: This is a constructor and does not return a value.
GameTime::GameTime(std::time_t system_ti.notes: The constructor uses a fixed reference offset (650336715 seconds) to align system time with the game's internal calendar. The sunlight phase is determined by specific hour thresholds, creating a realistic day-night cycle. The calculation relies on constants defining pulse rates and time units to maintain accurate synchronization with the game's timing system.
GameTime::GameTime(std::time_t system_ti.rationale: This constructor is used when initializing game time based on real-world system time, such as during game startup or synchronization events, to ensure the in-game environment reflects the current real-world time accurately.
GameTime::GameTime(std::time_t system_ti.name: GameTime

virtual GameTime::~GameTime().brief: Virtual destructor for the GameTime class to ensure proper cleanup of resources.
virtual GameTime::~GameTime().details: This destructor is declared as virtual to guarantee that the destructors of derived classes are invoked correctly when an object is deleted through a base class pointer. Although the destructor body is empty in this implementation, it establishes a proper interface for subclasses that may manage resources requiring explicit release. This design helps prevent resource leaks and undefined behavior during object destruction, especially in polymorphic use cases.
virtual GameTime::~GameTime().returns: This function does not return a value.
virtual GameTime::~GameTime().notes: Having a virtual destructor is essential in classes intended for inheritance to ensure correct destruction order. Even if the destructor body is empty, declaring it virtual maintains proper polymorphic behavior.
virtual GameTime::~GameTime().rationale: This destructor is used when a GameTime object or its derived classes are deleted through a base class pointer, ensuring that all resources are properly released and preventing potential resource leaks.
virtual GameTime::~GameTime().name: ~GameTime

void GameTime::update().brief: Advances the in-game time by one hour, updating date, sunlight state, and notifying relevant characters of key time events.
void GameTime::update().details: The GameTime::update function increments the current hour, and if it reaches or exceeds the maximum hours in a day (MUD_DAY), it resets the hour to zero and increments the day count. When the day count reaches the total days in a month (MUD_MONTH), it resets to zero and increments the month. Similarly, if the month reaches the total months in a year (MUD_YEAR), it resets to zero and increments the year. Based on the new hour, the function updates the sunlight state (e.g., Day, Sunrise, Sunset, Night) and constructs a message indicating significant time events such as sunrise, midday, sunset, and midnight. It then iterates over all characters in the game world, sending these notifications to those who are outside, awake, and have active tick flags, ensuring timely updates about the environment's state and time progression.
void GameTime::update().returns: This function does not return a value.
void GameTime::update().notes: The function triggers environmental and character notifications at specific hours, aligning game events with the simulated day-night cycle. It relies on constants defining the length of days, months, and years, ensuring consistent time progression. The notification messages are constructed dynamically based on the current hour, providing immersive cues to players about the time of day.
void GameTime::update().rationale: This function is used to simulate the passage of time within the game world, ensuring that environmental states and character notifications stay synchronized with the in-game clock. It is typically called at regular intervals (e.g., each game tick or real-time interval) to advance the game time and trigger associated events, maintaining an immersive and dynamic environment.
void GameTime::update().name: update

const String & GameTime::day_name() cons.brief: Returns the name of the current day in the game's weekly cycle based on the in-game day count.
const String & GameTime::day_name() cons.details: This function determines the current day's name by calculating the index as the current in-game day modulo 7, then returning the corresponding name from a static array of predefined day names. The use of a static array ensures that the day names are initialized only once, providing efficient access. The returned reference points to a static string, which avoids copying overhead. The day names are thematically linked to in-game events or lore, enhancing the immersive experience. This method ensures that the day name cycles correctly through the seven-day sequence regardless of how many days have passed in the game.
const String & GameTime::day_name() cons.returns: A reference to a String object representing the current day's name in the weekly cycle.
const String & GameTime::day_name() cons.notes: The function relies on the 'day' variable, which tracks the current in-game day within the month, to determine the correct day name. Since the array is static, the same set of names is reused across calls, ensuring consistency. The modulo operation guarantees proper cycling through the seven day names even as 'day' increases indefinitely.
const String & GameTime::day_name() cons.rationale: This function is used whenever the game needs to display or utilize the current day's name, such as in event scheduling, narrative descriptions, or user interface elements, providing contextual and thematic information aligned with the game's calendar system.
const String & GameTime::day_name() cons.name: day_name

const String GameTime::day_string() cons.brief: Returns the current in-game day as a string with the appropriate ordinal suffix.
const String GameTime::day_string() cons.details: This function calculates the current day by incrementing the zero-based 'day' variable to a one-based index. It then determines the correct ordinal suffix ('st', 'nd', 'rd', or 'th') based on English grammar rules, including special cases for days between 4 and 19 where 'th' is used regardless of the last digit. The resulting day number and suffix are formatted into a string using the Format::format function, producing a human-readable representation of the current day with proper ordinal notation suitable for display in game messages or user interfaces.
const String GameTime::day_string() cons.returns: A String object containing the current day with its ordinal suffix, formatted as a string.
const String GameTime::day_string() cons.notes: The function handles special ordinal suffix rules for days ending in 1, 2, and 3, except for days 11, 12, and 13, which always use 'th'. This ensures correct grammatical formatting for all days in the game calendar.
const String GameTime::day_string() cons.rationale: Used in scenarios where a human-readable, ordinal representation of the current game day is needed, such as displaying the date to players or generating descriptive logs that include the day of the month.
const String GameTime::day_string() cons.name: day_string

const String & GameTime::month_name() co.brief: Returns the name of the current month in the game's custom calendar.
const String & GameTime::month_name() co.details: This function retrieves the name of the current month by accessing a static array of predefined month names and returning a reference to the element corresponding to the internal month index. The month index is stored as an integer within the GameTime object and is assumed to be valid, ensuring safe array access. The static array is initialized once and reused across calls, making the function efficient. The month names are thematically chosen to reflect different periods within the game's world, providing meaningful context for time-dependent features. The function does not modify any state and provides a read-only view of the current month's name.
const String & GameTime::month_name() co.returns: A reference to a String object representing the current month's name.
const String & GameTime::month_name() co.throws: None; the function assumes the internal month index is within valid bounds.
const String & GameTime::month_name() co.notes: The static array of month names is shared across all instances and calls, ensuring minimal overhead. Proper validation of the month index should be handled elsewhere to prevent out-of-bounds access.
const String & GameTime::month_name() co.rationale: This function is used whenever the current month's name needs to be displayed or included in game messages, such as during updates, descriptions, or time displays, providing thematic consistency and immersion.
const String & GameTime::month_name() co.name: month_name
